title: lora_l2.c + lichen_l2.c: callback invocation timing not protected by state check
description:
Cross-file interaction bug: RX callback is invoked at lora_l2.c:539 without verifying module state hasn't changed.

lora_l2.c:515-518:
  k_mutex_lock(&lora_mutex, K_FOREVER);
  lichen_lora_rx_cb_t cb = lora_data.rx_callback;
  void *cb_user_data = lora_data.rx_callback_user_data;
  k_mutex_unlock(&lora_mutex);

lora_l2.c:520:
  if (cb) { cb(...); }

TOCTOU SCENARIO:
1. RX thread snapshots callback pointer at line 516-517
2. Thread releases mutex at line 518
3. Another thread calls stop() which clears callback at line 730-731
4. stop() sets state to STOPPED at line 734
5. RX thread invokes cb() at line 539 with now-stale callback

This is DOCUMENTED at lines 521-538 (callback interruption risk). The design accepts that in-flight callbacks complete even after stop begins.

However, the callback (lora_rx_callback -> lichen_l2_input) doesn't recheck lora state inside lichen_l2.c. It only checks link_ctx_initialized and iface_init_failed.

If the state transitions to ABORTED (not just STOPPED) after callback snapshot but before lichen_l2_input() processes, the input code runs on a module that needs reinit.

Actually lichen_l2.c:1790 checks link_ctx_initialized which is NOT cleared during stop() - only during disable(). And line 1420 checks iface_init_failed.

ISSUE: needs_reinit() (ABORTED state) is not checked in lichen_l2_input() after acquiring mutex. If ABORTED is set between callback snapshot and mutex acquire, processing continues on corrupt state.

FIX: Add needs_reinit() check at lichen_l2.c:1783 after acquiring rx_mutex.
